# Alignment Checklist: Search Quality Race CI

**Purpose**: Validate that the Search Quality Race CI specification fully and unambiguously covers the requested CI, audit, reporting, issue-collection, and validation requirements.  
**Created**: 2026-05-16  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are nightly and manual trigger requirements both documented with enough scope to distinguish recurring quality monitoring from ad hoc validation runs? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK002 Are manual override requirements complete for seed, sample size, competitor package selection, and simulated-failure mode? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §SC-014]
- [x] CHK003 Are repository workflow responsibilities separated clearly enough between quality failures and infrastructure failures? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018, Spec §SC-013]
- [x] CHK004 Are current-repository iDocs evaluation requirements stated separately from public competitor-release evaluation requirements? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK005 Is the default competitor set specified with enough detail to avoid ambiguity in the benchmark baseline? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK006 Are remote-only CI requirements complete, including intentional local documentation unavailability, retained diagnostics, and explicit local-comparison exclusion? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK007 Are audit-pool data requirements complete for all requested case fields, including IDs, query metadata, expected outcome, canonical expectations, eligibility, and runner hints? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK008 Are initial framework and product-area coverage requirements complete for SwiftUI, AppKit, UIKit, Foundation, Xcode, and App Store Connect? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK009 Are initial query-shape coverage requirements complete for exact-symbol, composite, member-property, natural-language, fuzzy-typo, and invalid/no-result cases? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK010 Are report deliverable requirements complete across machine-readable artifact, human-readable artifact, and run summary surfaces? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-020, Spec §FR-021]
- [x] CHK011 Are issue-collection requirements complete for no-op, create-new-issue, update-existing-issue, label best-effort, and simulated-failure validation paths? [Completeness, Spec §FR-024 through FR-030]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK012 Is "public competitor release" clarified enough to exclude source checkout comparison while still allowing manual package selection overrides? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK013 Is "same seeded stratified random pool" defined clearly enough to determine reproducibility, eligibility filtering, and sampling order expectations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK014 Are classification values defined with stable meanings rather than only listed as names? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, Spec §Key Entities, Ambiguity]
- [x] CHK015 Are verdict values defined with stable meanings and clear boundaries between quality failure, infrastructure failure, and not-applicable outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018]
- [x] CHK016 Is the golden-truth failure concept defined clearly enough to distinguish iDocs product regressions from competitor failures and network noise? [Clarity, Spec §FR-024, Spec §User Story 4]
- [x] CHK017 Is the fingerprint input definition clear enough to guarantee case-order stability and avoid duplicate issues for equivalent failure sets? [Clarity, Spec §FR-025, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK018 Are iDocs failure-row requirements specific enough to ensure maintainers get case, query, expectation, classification, top evidence, and reproduction context in one place? [Clarity, Spec §FR-022, Spec §SC-009]
- [x] CHK019 Is the remote-only diagnostic requirement specific enough to require a machine-readable local-documentation-unavailable signal without treating local cache behavior as a race dimension? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK020 Are quality-failure handling requirements consistent between user scenarios, functional requirements, and success criteria? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-017, Spec §SC-013]
- [x] CHK021 Are infrastructure-failure requirements consistent across build, package-resolution, runner, report, artifact, and required issue-collection failure cases? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Spec §SC-013]
- [x] CHK022 Are issue no-op requirements consistent between the no-failure scenario and the explicit functional requirement? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-029, Spec §SC-010]
- [x] CHK023 Are label best-effort requirements consistent between edge cases and issue-collection functional requirements? [Consistency, Spec §FR-028, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK024 Are remote-only assumptions consistent with the Apple-iDocs CLI-first boundary and do they avoid reintroducing MCP/runtime service obligations into the product? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-015, Assumption]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK025 Are success criteria measurable for every major deliverable: scheduling, reproducible sampling, eligibility filtering, classification, reporting, artifacts, issue creation, and duplicate prevention? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001 through SC-014]
- [x] CHK026 Are the requested report sections mapped to measurable success criteria rather than only functional requirements? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-021 through FR-023, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-008, Spec §SC-009]
- [x] CHK027 Are no-failure and repeated-failure issue outcomes measurable with 100% expectations? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-010, Spec §SC-011, Spec §SC-012]
- [x] CHK028 Is the simulated-failure validation path measurable enough to support issue create/update proof without relying on a real regression? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-030, Spec §SC-014]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK029 Are primary flows covered for scheduled monitoring, manual validation, cross-product comparison, report review, issue collection, and remote-only CI interpretation? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1 through 5]
- [x] CHK030 Are alternate flows covered for manual package overrides, no iDocs failures, repeated fingerprints, missing preferred labels, and simulated failures? [Coverage, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-026 through FR-030, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK031 Are exception flows covered for competitor install failure, iDocs build failure, runner failure, report-generation failure, artifact-publication failure, and issue-collection failure? [Coverage, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK032 Are product-result scenario classes complete for pass, fail, infrastructure, and not-applicable verdicts across iDocs and competitors? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK033 Are classification scenario classes complete for symbol hits, module-only results, empty results, wrong framework, wrong page, unsupported or misclassified results, and network errors? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK034 Are eligibility and undersized-sample edge cases specified clearly enough to avoid accidental duplicate sampling or hidden sample shrinkage? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK035 Are invalid/no-result cases and known canonical-document cases differentiated enough that empty results are judged correctly in both contexts? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-013, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK036 Are module-only result exceptions specified clearly enough to preserve framework/module control cases without weakening symbol/member expectations? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-014, Spec §SC-006]
- [x] CHK037 Are network-error and infrastructure-noise exclusions from issue creation specified for both iDocs and competitor outputs? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-024, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK038 Are sensitive-value redaction expectations specified for summaries, artifacts, issue bodies, and comments? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK039 Are assumptions documented for npm-release competitor evaluation, non-gating quality monitoring, remote-only CI interpretation, default sample size, and default competitor set? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK040 Are external dependency expectations documented for public package availability and repository issue permissions without over-specifying implementation mechanics? [Dependency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-027]
- [x] CHK041 Are workflow operational constraints that materially affect requirements, such as repository write permission for issue collection, documented without embedding low-level workflow syntax? [Dependency, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-027]
- [x] CHK042 Are implementation-specific deliverables from the user request explicitly deferred to planning only where they do not change requirement behavior? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK043 Is the expected behavior for report-generation success but issue-collection permission failure unambiguous enough to classify the run consistently? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK044 Is "required issue collection" scoped clearly enough to distinguish optional label application from mandatory issue create/update/no-op behavior? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-028]
- [x] CHK045 Is the boundary between "competitor package selection override" and "default competitor set" clear enough for manual runs? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK046 Are any implementation details intentionally omitted from the spec documented as plan-phase concerns rather than accidental omissions? [Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- This checklist validates requirements alignment, not implementation behavior.
- Validation passed after `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/`, `quickstart.md`, and `tasks.md` were generated.
